title: Icarus Robotics taps KULR to provide batteries for Space Station robots
summary: Icarus Robotics has selected KULR Technology Group to supply batteries for Joy, a free-flying robot platform intended for space station missions. This collaboration highlights advancements in space robotics and battery technology.

title: Laying the groundwork for the first human mission to Mars
summary: The article discusses the complexities of planning the first human mission to Mars, highlighting the numerous challenges involved in safely sending humans to the Red Planet and returning them to Earth.

title: Satellite investment sets annual record halfway through 2026
summary: Investment in satellite companies hit $8.1 billion in the first half of 2026, already exceeding the total annual investment recorded in previous years. This marks a significant milestone in early-stage funding for the space industry.

title: Japan seeks to ramp up launch activity
summary: Japan aims to significantly increase its launch activities despite facing challenges with both existing and new launch vehicles.

title: Isaacman attends Soyuz launch of ISS crew
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman attended the Soyuz launch of a new crew to the International Space Station on July 14. The crew successfully arrived at the ISS following the launch.

title: Space acquisition and NRO picks face SASC
summary: Erich Hernandez-Baquero and Roger Mason, nominated for key space and NRO leadership roles, testified before the Senate Armed Services Committee. Their nominations are under review for critical positions in U.S. space acquisition and intelligence.
company: U.S. Air Force and National Reconnaissance Office

title: Antaris™ Establishes Aeonyx™ to Advance All-Domain Mission Virtualization for Defense
summary: Antaris has created a new company, Aeonyx, to advance mission virtualization for defense applications. This strategic move aims to help defense organizations evaluate architectures and improve operational performance.

title: U.K. government preparing to release new space strategy
summary: The British government is set to unveil a new space strategy that emphasizes a coordinated 'whole-of-government' approach to space activities. This initiative aims to streamline and enhance the country's space sector policies.

title: Flexell Space raises $20 Million in Series A round
summary: Flexell Space, a developer of next-generation space solar solutions, raised $20 million in a Series A funding round. The company is based in Uiwang, South Korea.

title: Defense spending lifts Europe’s space economy
summary: European government space spending increased by 12% to $15.4 billion in 2025, driven by rising defense budgets despite a 3% global decline. This trend is highlighted in a July 13 European Space Agency report.

title: 'Found you!' Astronomers spot faintest exoplanet ever seen from Earth after a decade of hide-and-seek
summary: Astronomers have discovered the faintest exoplanet ever observed from Earth, located around the star Beta Pictoris, after a decade-long search. This breakthrough highlights advancements in exoplanet detection techniques.

title: Every SpaceX Starlink satellite has to dodge a collision almost weekly, and experts fear the worst
summary: SpaceX's Starlink satellites performed over 355,000 collision avoidance maneuvers in the past year, with each satellite dodging other objects nearly weekly. Experts express growing concerns about the long-term risks of such frequent evasive actions.

title: What time is SpaceX's Starship Flight 13 launch on July 16? (Full mission timeline)
summary: SpaceX is scheduled to launch its 13th Starship test flight on July 16, carrying the first V3 Starlink satellites. The mission marks a significant step in SpaceX's development of its next-generation rocket and satellite constellation.

title: SpaceX launches flight-proven rocket for 600th time, sending Starlink satellites to orbit (video)
summary: SpaceX achieved the 600th reuse of a flight-proven rocket by launching Starlink satellites on two Falcon 9 missions within eight hours. The launches were conducted from opposite coasts on July 13-14, 2026.

title: Scientists spot 4 superdense stellar corpses hiding behind their red dwarf companions
summary: Scientists have discovered four superdense stellar remnants, known as white dwarfs, hidden behind their red dwarf companions. These isolated white dwarfs were previously undetectable due to their obscured visibility.

title: The 1st of 10,000 'missing' black holes in the Omega Centauri star cluster has been found by the Hubble and James Webb space telescopes
summary: The Hubble and James Webb space telescopes have discovered the first of 10,000 'missing' black holes in the Omega Centauri star cluster. This finding helps address a long-standing astronomical mystery.

title: 'Once Upon a Time in Space': How to watch PBS's out-of-this-world astronaut documentary
summary: PBS released a documentary titled 'Once Upon a Time in Space' featuring astronauts, described as a visually stunning journey akin to 'Oz'. The film highlights the beauty and wonder of space exploration.

title: 1st-ever X-rays in space offer hope for possible patients headed to the moon
summary: A portable X-ray device has been successfully used in orbit for the first time, offering potential medical and technical applications for future lunar missions. This breakthrough could enable X-ray diagnosis of injuries or equipment damage in space.

title: This cosmic 'lighthouse' is blazing a magnetic trail through the Milky Way
summary: Astronomers have directly mapped the magnetic field around an unusual pulsar in the Milky Way, confirming a decades-old prediction about particle streaming from the rapidly spinning stellar remnant.

title: Honeycomb structures spotted on Mars | Space photo of the day for July 14, 2026
summary: A space photo from July 14, 2026, shows honeycomb structures on Mars, sparking curiosity about their origin. The image may depict natural geological formations or potential signs of artificial construction.

title: Volunteer Measures Record Louisiana Rainfall
summary: Volunteers are measuring record rainfall in Louisiana to contribute data for scientific research and weather forecasting. This effort supports a national community of precipitation reporters.

title: NASA’s Webb Discovers Hidden Planet in Famous Star System
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope discovered a hidden giant exoplanet in the Beta Pictoris star system, which was already known to host two other giant planets. This finding adds to the growing body of knowledge about planetary systems in our galaxy.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4947-4953: Gale Crater Then and Now
summary: The Curiosity rover continues its exploration of Gale Crater, moving through distinct geological units. The mission is planned and executed by scientists at York University, Toronto.

title: Heat Dome Broils the Western U.S.
summary: A high-pressure ridge caused record-breaking temperatures in Montana, Utah, and Wyoming on July 12, 2026. The extreme heat, described as a 'heat dome,' posed challenges for infrastructure and public health.

title: NASA Invites Media to Serbia Artemis Accords Signing Ceremony
summary: The Republic of Serbia is set to sign the Artemis Accords in a ceremony hosted by NASA at its headquarters in Washington. The event will be attended by NASA Deputy Administrator Matt Anderson, Serbia’s Minister of Foreign Affairs Marko Đurić, and U.S. State Department officials.

title: NASA Astronaut Anil Menon, Crewmates Arrive at Space Station
summary: NASA astronaut Anil Menon and two Roscosmos cosmonauts arrived at the International Space Station aboard the Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft. The mission increased the station's crew to 10 members for the next two weeks.

title: Hubble Sees Crimson Cloud and Stars
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured a striking image of stellar nursery LH 95 in the Large Magellanic Cloud, showcasing crimson gas and blue-white stars. The region features low-mass infant stars alongside massive blue giants.

title: NASA Jets Turn Red, White, and Blue
summary: NASA's F-15 and F/A-18 jets were repainted in red, white, and blue to celebrate America's 250th birthday. The aircraft will participate in a flyover in Washington on July 4, 2026.

title: NASA’s Roman Telescope Will Spot Distant Black Holes That Shred Stars
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope will help scientists study distant black holes that shred stars to understand their formation and growth over time. The telescope aims to detect supermassive black holes from the early universe.

title: Soyuz MS-29 launches new crew to ISS from Baikonur
summary: The Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft successfully launched a new crew to the International Space Station (ISS) from the Baikonur Cosmodrome on July 14. This marks the latest crewed mission to the orbital laboratory.

title: Launch Preview: Soyuz to launch crew to ISS, Starship to fly another test flight
summary: Soyuz is set to launch a crew to the International Space Station (ISS), while SpaceX's Starship is preparing for its 13th flight test. Seven launches are planned globally this week.

title: NASA’s Roman telescope into prelaunch servicing following arrival in Florida
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has arrived at Kennedy Space Center for prelaunch servicing. This marks a key step before its planned launch.

title: Booster 20 completes record duration Static Fire
summary: SpaceX completed a record duration static fire test for Booster 20 using 33 engines. This marks a significant milestone in the company's development efforts.

title: Rocket Lab sees Electron gains, Neutron delays in first half of 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket achieved record performance in the first half of 2026, while the development of its Neutron rocket faced delays.

title: Progress at the Cape: New Launch Pads rise for Stoke, Relativity
summary: The Space Coast is seeing significant progress with new launch pads being constructed for Stoke Space and Relativity Space. This development highlights the ongoing revitalization of the region's space industry infrastructure.

title: Launch Preview: Long March 10B and Vikram-I debut
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled internationally this week, including the debut of China's Long March 10B and India's Vikram-I rockets.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 space station is progressing toward a planned Q1 2027 launch, having achieved key milestones. The station represents a significant step in commercial space station development.

title: Starship in Florida pushing for launch this year
summary: SpaceX is accelerating preparations for Starship launches from Cape Canaveral, aiming for a launch this year. The company and its contractors are making progress toward this goal.

title: NASA announces new lander contracts for Moon Base Phase One
summary: NASA announced four new lunar landers as part of its Moon Base Phase One initiative. The contracts aim to support future lunar exploration and base construction efforts.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry news item, but no specific details were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting events to discuss the implementation of decisions from the CM25 ministerial meeting, focusing on industrial opportunities and thematic priorities across ESA programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: A new advanced robotic arm technology is being developed for space applications. This innovation aims to enhance robotic operations in space missions.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme enables European research institutes to participate in space science missions. The latest success is the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission launch in Norway.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on young researchers to test and validate materials for spacecraft assembly, ensuring they can withstand the harsh conditions of space. This team brings innovative ideas to space-proof ESA's missions.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news discusses a sturdy and sensitive technology, though specific details are not provided in the given text.

title: Exclusive: Loft Orbital Scales Offering with Apex Bus Purchase
summary: Loft Orbital acquired new satellite bus inventory to support larger and more power-intensive customer payloads. The purchase aims to expand their offerings in the space industry.

title: NatSec Space Policy Nominees Face Questions from Senators
summary: Pentagon's space acquisition nominee testified that legacy military space programs are incompatible with modern acquisition reforms. Senators questioned nominees on space policy during a hearing.

title: Payload Field Guide: NSSL
summary: The U.S. Space Force expanded its National Security Space Launch (NSSL) program by adding two new launch providers on July 8, bringing the total number of companies in the program to seven.

title: Spinifex Space Launches With Australian Test Ranges
summary: Spinifex Space has launched operations to offer end-to-end suborbital launch campaigns, private range access, and test and evaluation infrastructure. The company will utilize Australian test ranges for these services.

title: Lunar Outpost Joins ESA’s Moonraker Mission in 2030
summary: Lunar Outpost Europe has joined ESA's Moonraker mission, scheduled for 2030, to develop thermal management solutions for the spacecraft. The collaboration aims to address the challenges of extreme temperature variations in lunar orbit.

title: Reditus Finishes ENOS Reentry Vehicle Ahead of First Flight
summary: Reditus has completed the development of its first reentry spacecraft, ENOS, ahead of its scheduled debut flight this fall.

title: Reflect Orbital Cleared to Fly In-Orbit Mirror by FCC
summary: Reflect Orbital has received FCC clearance to launch an in-orbit mirror, despite concerns about its environmental and astronomical impact. The FCC stated that such concerns fall outside its regulatory scope.

title: Pulse Space Lands $40M Space Force Contract to Develop Laser Tech
summary: Pulse Space, a Washington-based startup, secured a $40 million contract from the U.S. Space Force to develop laser technology for beaming power to satellites in orbit. The funding will advance their innovative laser systems designed for in-space energy transfer.

title: US Government Studying Offshore Launch Opportunities
summary: The U.S. government is exploring offshore launch opportunities by studying the feasibility of building or repurposing infrastructure on the Outer Continental Shelf for space launches and reentries. The Bureau of Ocean Energy Management (BOEM) issued a Request for Information (RFI) to gather relevant data.

title: NATO Allies Plan to Stitch Together Sovereign Space Fleets
summary: Eight NATO allies are planning to integrate their national defense satellites into a unified network. This initiative aims to enhance collective security through shared space-based capabilities.

title: Honeywell to Lead ESA Quantum Space Magnetometer Contract
summary: Honeywell Aerospace will lead a consortium to develop a compact quantum space magnetometer for Earth's magnetic field research. The project involves partners Quantum Brilliance and Jagiellonian University.

title: NRL Awards Parsons $245M Satellite Ground Systems Contract for Tracking Facility
summary: The U.S. Naval Research Laboratory awarded Parsons Corporation's subsidiary a $245 million contract to support satellite ground systems software and operations at a tracking facility. The contract continues Parsons' existing work in this domain.

title: Frontier to Adopt Starlink With New Customer Portal From SpaceX
summary: Frontier Airlines and other Indigo Partners airlines will adopt SpaceX's Starlink in-flight connectivity for over 1,000 aircraft. The deal includes a new customer portal for managing the service.

title: Iridium’s PNT Chip is Now Commercially Available
summary: Iridium has made its positioning, navigation, and timing (PNT) chip commercially available. Over 150 organizations have shown interest in the chip since its announcement.

title: L3Harris and Sierra Space Receive $1.75B for SDA Tranche 3, Tracking Layer
summary: L3Harris Technologies and Sierra Space secured $1.75 billion in contracts for the SDA Tranche 3, Tracking Layer satellites. The funds will be used for expedited launches under fixed-price OTAs.

title: The Implications of Dual-Use for Protecting Space Assets
summary: Europe has historically separated civil space programs from defense, with clear distinctions between commercial, institutional, and government roles. However, the rise of dual-use technologies is challenging these traditional divisions.

title: Ravee Optics Secures $6 Million Seed Round to Build Compact Laser Terminals
summary: Ravee Optics, a startup developing compact laser terminals, has secured $6 million in an oversubscribed seed funding round. The company aims to advance its technology in the space industry.

title: Sonatel Taps Eutelsat OneWeb for Teleport Upgrade
summary: Sonatel, Senegal's leading telecommunications company, has upgraded its teleport in collaboration with Eutelsat OneWeb. The upgrade aims to enhance connectivity services in a country with over 19 million people.

title: FCC Approves Reflect Orbital Demo Satellite to Test Redirecting Sunlight
summary: The FCC has approved Reflect Orbital's demonstration satellite to test redirecting sunlight from orbit back to Earth using a solar reflector. This marks a step forward in space-based solar power and light reflection technologies.

title: Space42 and Skylo Test D2D Test on Thuraya-4
summary: Space42 and Skylo successfully tested direct-to-device messaging, including SMS and SOS, on the Thuraya-4 satellite. The partners announced the achievement on July 9 and plan to expand the service.
